title: Where Are the Republicans Who Put America First?
description: The “Trump First” wing of the G.O.P. is in ascendance. The rest of the party needs to fight back.
author: Thomas L. Friedman

title: I.R.S. to Drop Audits of Trump and Family
description: As part of the Justice Department’s compensation fund deal, officials vowed not to pursue any matters, including those involving President Trump’s tax returns, that are pending.
author: Alan Feuer, Andrew Duehren and Glenn Thrush

title: Woman Dies After Falling Into Open Manhole in Manhattan
description: The woman plunged 10 feet, and a passer-by heard her screams. Con Edison said video footage suggested the manhole cover had been dislodged by a truck 12 minutes earlier.
author: Ashley Southall

title: Trump’s Special Envoy to Greenland Receives a Cold Welcome From Locals
description: After President Trump’s threats to seize the island, Gov. Jeff Landry’s offers of MAGA hats and chocolate chip cookies fall flat.
author: Maya Tekeli and Jeffrey Gettleman

title: Trump Endorses Ken Paxton in Texas Senate Runoff Race Against John Cornyn
description: The president embraced Ken Paxton, a MAGA loyalist, over Senator John Cornyn, despite warnings from Republican leaders about Mr. Paxton’s history of scandal.
author: Shane Goldmacher

title: Republican Senators Are Livid at Trump’s Endorsement of Paxton
description: News that President Trump had snubbed Senator John Cornyn of Texas, the four-term incumbent, was met with shock, anger and fear that the G.O.P. could lose his seat.
author: Michael Gold, Megan Mineiro and Carl Hulse

title: ‘The Future of Truth’ Contains Quotes Made Up by A.I.
description: Steven Rosenbaum, author of “The Future of Truth,” said he had started his own investigation after The New York Times asked about the fake quotes.
author: Benjamin Mullin

title: The IRS Thought it Could Fight Trump’s Lawsuit, but it Struck a Deal Anyway
description: Officials wrote a memo outlining ways to challenge President Trump’s suit against the Internal Revenue Service. The administration is instead creating a compensation fund.
author: Andrew Duehren

title: Trump’s Approval Sinks Amid Unpopular War, Darkening G.O.P. Prospects
description: With the midterms nearing, President Trump’s approval rating has hit a second-term low as voters question his handling of the economy, according to the latest New York Times/Siena poll.
author: Lisa Lerer, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker

title: Top Treasury Lawyer Resigns After Creation of ‘Anti-Weaponization Fund’
description: Brian Morrissey, the department’s general counsel, stepped down hours after the Trump administration announced the $1.8 billion fund.
author: Andrew Duehren

title: Judge Bars ICE From Making Immigration Arrests at Courts in New York
description: The Trump administration’s practice of detaining migrants at federal courthouses had stirred outrage.
author: Jonah E. Bromwich and Benjamin Weiser

title: Justice Dept. Sets Up $1.8 Billion Fund That Could Funnel Money to Trump Allies
description: The arrangement was denounced by critics as a slush fund for supporters of President Trump, possibly including Jan. 6 rioters.
author: Glenn Thrush, Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer

title: E.P.A. to Repeal Some Limits on ‘Forever Chemicals’ in Drinking Water
description: The rules were established by the Biden administration after research linked the compounds to a range of serious health problems.
author: Lisa Friedman

title: Trump Has Done More Than Misjudge Iran
description: The war has revealed the true puniness of Trump’s power
author: Lydia Polgreen

title: Manager at Trump’s N.J. Golf Club Helped Plan Reflecting Pool Repairs
description: David Schutzenhofer, a Trump Organization employee, reached out to one of the vendors the government later hired for the iconic site near the Lincoln Memorial, documents show.
author: David A. Fahrenthold and Maxine Joselow

title: N.Y.C. Hotel Housekeepers Will Earn Over $100,000 Under New Contract
description: A deal between a powerful union and an industry group substantially boosts annual pay for workers coping with New York City’s high cost of living.
author: Patrick McGeehan

title: Trump’s Deportations Are Costing Americans Jobs, Study Finds
description: According to a new study, construction was impacted more than any other industry studied, with American-born workers losing more jobs than immigrants as a result of the deportations.
author: Ronda Kaysen

title: Why College Grads Are Booing Their Commencement Speakers
description: The generation that grew up with A.I. hates it.
author: Michelle Goldberg

title: Neanderthal Dentistry, and the Scientist Glad Not to Have Experienced It
description: The prehistoric hominins “apparently were very adept at what we would consider invasive medicine,” said the anthropologist John Olsen.
author: Franz Lidz

title: Iran War Exposes Shortcomings in U.S. Military Industrial Base
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is taking on a problem that the Pentagon and Congress have tried, and mostly failed, to address for years.
author: Julian E. Barnes
